novel_features.name: Segment Effort Progression
novel_features.description: See your entire effort history on a segment — date, time, avg power, avg HR, delta from PR — so you can track if you're actually improving.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining all segment efforts with their stream data in local SQLite; Strava web only shows your PR.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to assess whether an athlete is progressing on a target training segment over a season.

novel_features.name: Training Load Timeline (CTL/ATL/TSB)
novel_features.description: See your Chronic Training Load, Acute Training Load, and Training Stress Balance as sparklines so you can spot overtraining or undertaper before a race.
novel_features.rationale: Requires computing exponential decay aggregations over historical TSS values stored in local SQLite — no single Strava API call provides this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to assess an athlete's fitness/fatigue state and readiness for an upcoming event.

novel_features.name: Zone Time Distribution
novel_features.description: See how many minutes per week you actually spent in each heart rate or power zone, so you can tell if your training distribution matches your plan.
novel_features.rationale: Requires decoding second-by-second stream data for each activity and cross-referencing athlete zone thresholds from the API — no single call provides this aggregate.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when evaluating whether a training block was executed as prescribed (polarized, sweet-spot, base).

novel_features.name: Power Curve
novel_features.description: See your best mean power for every standard duration (1s to 60min) so you can identify strengths, weaknesses, and fitness changes across seasons.
novel_features.rationale: Requires sliding-window max over per-second watts arrays across all synced activities — computationally intensive local operation that no Strava API endpoint provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to characterize a cyclist's physiological profile or compare peak power across training blocks.

novel_features.name: HR Drift Detector
novel_features.description: Measure aerobic decoupling in an activity — the ratio of HR rise to pace drop in the second half — to assess aerobic fitness without lab testing.
novel_features.rationale: Requires splitting heartrate and velocity_smooth streams at the temporal midpoint and computing normalized drift — a multi-stream local calculation not available from any Strava endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to identify which long runs or rides showed aerobic decoupling, indicating the athlete was above their aerobic threshold.

novel_features.name: Bulk Activity Updater
novel_features.description: Update gear, name template, or description across hundreds of activities at once with a preview-before-commit safety net.
novel_features.rationale: Requires filtering activities from local SQLite and issuing batched PUT requests with built-in rate-limit management — the Strava web interface has no bulk operation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to mass-migrate a gear assignment after equipment replacement or retroactively organize a training block's activities.

novel_features.name: Gear Retirement Tracker
novel_features.description: See total mileage on each shoe and bike, your configured replacement threshold, and an estimated retirement date based on your recent usage rate.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating activity distance by gear_id across local SQLite and projecting forward based on weekly average — no Strava API call computes this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to check whether any gear is approaching retirement before an important race.

novel_features.name: KOM Gap Tracker
novel_features.description: See exactly how far you are from the KOM on each starred segment, ranked by the gap you're most likely to close.
novel_features.rationale: Requires combining your best local effort time with a live leaderboard fetch for each starred segment — a join of local and live data no single API call provides.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to surface the most achievable KOM targets for a training plan or pre-ride goal setting.

narrative.headline: Every Strava feature, plus offline analytics — training load, power curves, zone time, and segment progression — that the website can't show you.
narrative.value_prop: strava-pp-cli syncs your entire Strava history to a local SQLite database, then unlocks the analytics your training actually requires: CTL/ATL training load, power curve, zone time distribution, aerobic decoupling, and segment effort progression. Everything works offline, composes with jq, and speaks JSON natively for AI coaching agents.
narrative.auth_narrative: Strava uses OAuth2. Run `strava-pp-cli auth login` to open the browser authorization page — the CLI handles the local callback, token exchange, and storage automatically. Set STRAVA_CLIENT_ID and STRAVA_CLIENT_SECRET before running. Tokens are refreshed automatically; run `auth refresh` to force a refresh.

quickstart.comment: Verify credentials and API connectivity (run auth login first if not authenticated)
Verify credentials and check API connectivity
Mirror your Strava data to a local SQLite database
See your CTL/ATL/TSB training load timeline
Compute your power curve from synced stream data
Track your progression on a specific segment over time

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login fails with redirect_uri mismatch
troubleshoots.fix: Set STRAVA_REDIRECT_URI to the value configured in your Strava API application settings (default: http://localhost:8421/callback)
Strava rate-limits at 200 requests/15min. Run sync --slow to add a 1s delay between requests, or wait for the 15-minute window to reset
Power data requires sync with --streams flag: run sync --streams to fetch per-second watts data. Requires activities recorded with a power meter
TSS computation uses Strava's suffer_score by default. For power-based TSS supply --ftp <watts> and run sync --streams first
Re-run with --rate-limit-sleep 4 to spread requests across the 15-min window. The command is idempotent — already-updated activities are skipped

narrative.when_to_use: Use strava-pp-cli when an agent needs to query an athlete's historical training data, compute fitness metrics unavailable from the Strava web interface, manage activities in bulk, or build a training analysis pipeline. It is the right choice when the question involves aggregating across more than 5 activities, involves stream-level data (heart rate, power, GPS), or requires offline access to Strava data.

recipes.title: Check training readiness before a race
recipes.explanation: Shows TSB (freshness) for each of the past 8 weeks; negative TSB = fatigue, positive = fresh. Ideal for race week targeting TSB > 10.

recipes.title: Find your best power efforts this year
recipes.explanation: Returns best mean power per standard window normalized to W/kg — share with a coach for physiological profiling.

recipes.title: Identify aerobically decoupled long runs
recipes.explanation: Lists all runs where HR drifted more than 8% relative to pace in the second half — these are training days where you went too hard.

recipes.title: See your segment progression this season
recipes.explanation: Shows every effort on segment 229781, ordered by date, with delta from your PR so you can see the trend clearly.

recipes.title: Check gear retirement status before a marathon
recipes.explanation: Shows all running gear with mileage and estimated retirement date; use --select to narrow to the fields a race-day checklist needs.
